description: Helping meeting to stay on track and professional is really amazing!
author: Shade O.
pros: The Agenda and the time-clock while a meeting is in prigrsss is one of the first features to catch my eye! How amazing is that?? Thus allows you to run a truly professional meeting where you stay on top of things and keep to time! This can really revolutionize meetings! Having a central hub also for users to go back and review past recordings is another notable feature that caught my eye. Digging further, I came across the ability to group your attendees into groups which can help create your own hub! This is incredible!
cons: Nothing! It is so intuitive that you can practically dive in and put it to use immediately! Well done to the team! 👏🏽
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: We have loved nucleus from the very beginning. Installing the launcher on our website as a central hub allows people to sign up, give, and stay up to date with all with one login for church admins, and attendees as well!
author: Andrew L.
pros: I love the low rate on donation fees. It's the lowest you'll find anywhere. When you consider everything it includes, Nucleus offers CHMS, email, stock footage, social posts, giving, event registration, website builder and a sermon engine all for $199 month.
cons: They lack third party integration with a lot of major church softwares. You have to manually export CSV files in order to migrate people or update information. They also deposit online giving once or twice a day, which makes reconciliation a bit tedious.

author: Adam W.
pros: Easy to use and simply to help reach your community for Jesus. Highly recommend.
cons: It too a few minutes to learn - but not too hard for the average tech savvy.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: Amazing church software created by church people for church people
description: I have loved Nucleus from the very start and my love for it continues to grow as well. The Nucleus team offers very quick support and is always very helpful. They also make products that are very easy for volunteers to use and do not require a steep learning curve.
author: Nathan N.
pros: I personally value the theology and philosophy that Nucleus incorporates into their products. They have concrete reasons why they develop certain features and give church leaders the ability the tools to do carry out their responsibilities simply and effectively.
cons: Since Nucleus is so simple and effective for church purposes, it would be amazing if they were able to incorporate other aspects of Church Management Software.

review_title: Nucleus is a no-brainer for small to mid sized churches.
description: Phenomenal. Top notch customer service. Incredible team. [sensitive content hidden] is a visionary and we leave a long lasting mark on the Kingdom.
author: Jay S.
pros: The ease of use. I can train several volunteers to run Nucleus because they have simplified it for the church. Their platform is driven by the mission to be invitational instead of just inspirational.
cons: I wish I didn't have to use other platforms to manage my people or schedule services (planning center) but it makes sense because I've been able to condense my web platform to stay with Nucleus. I used to use squarespace so that at least helped me consolidate that aspect of our platforms.

review_title: You owe yourself the administrative relief that Nucleus provides.
author: Josh P.
pros: Cutting edge, user-first experience (for both church members and staff/volunteers). Saves time. Saves money. It just works.
cons: Would love to see Nucleus play better with other ChMS providers (two-way sync is a big need).

review_title: THE BEST all-in-one suite of website, social media, donation management products (+ beyond) around!
description: Again, clean, sleek, easy to use. And the team is top notch — helping my church any and every way they possibly could. I am very grateful for products and companies like Nucleus. You cannot go wrong with using Nucleus.
author: Rafael C.
pros: Nucleus is hands down the best all-in-one suite of website, social media, donation management (+ beyond) around! I personally didn’t utilize the donation management side of things in my last church, but if we hadn’t already been so deeply rooted in utilizing Planning Center products, I would’ve had us make that switch in a flash. Clean, sleek, incredibly easy to use.
cons: I honestly cannot say I disliked anything during my period of use within my last ministry. My only “regret” is having to stop using it.

description: Was the best thing we've done for our church.
author: David L.
pros: I could rant and rave about every feature that has enabled significant growth in the last two years, but I'll say this instead: Nucleus saved us so much money, we hired another full time pastor. A 110-person church has two full-time pastors, all because Nucleus enabled our church and community to take more next steps and save on giving fees.
cons: Nucleus is meant for the small church to thrive- if you manage the website and communication for a large church ministry, you may be disappointed in the lack of customization across Nucleus products. But that's because Nucleus puts wise guardrails up for users who are running small ministries and need time saved or to make it easy for volunteers to manage the website and management stuff.

description: Our website is better. The value for the money is incredible. The customer service is unmatchable.
author: Addison R.
pros: I love how easy it is. I can hand it off to someone with a minimal knowledge and they can build out a web page. Just this week a new team member built a landing page for her new ministry.
cons: The customization can be limited in service of functionality when compared to something like Divi
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

description: My overall experience with Nucleus has been predominantly favourable, especially regarding its functionality and adaptability.
author: Christy O.
pros: One of the aspects I appreciate most about Nucleus is its intuitive interface, which effectively simplifies and consolidates a range of business operations.
cons: One aspect of Nucleus that I find least favourable is its potential intricacy regarding customization and configuration.

review_title: The Premiere Choice for Church Web Development
author: Gabe S.
pros: After some experience with other web platforms (wix, Squarespace, Clover Sites, etc.) Nucleus Web has consistently provided the best possible product, especially with their new Web2 platform, they've developed amazing new tools that help with not only creating a highly-customizable website, but also a website that can be easily managed by a team of people -all with different levels of permissions. The quality of the output vs. the ease of use for a volunteer team makes this product a no brainer for almost any church.
cons: I have had very VERY few frustrations with Nucleus, their support team is out-of-this-world and they have treated our church in particular extremely well. I've never felt let down by the team, but the only thing that has consistently irritated me about the product is a lack of integrations with other software platforms, such as Planning Center or Mailchimp, specifically in the area of people/database management. For now at least, those three platforms exist separately and maintain their own people databases without syncing across, which causes a lot of extra work for church communications to make sure that everyone is included in an email campaign or to ensure we have someone's correct contact info.

review_title: Nucleus is and exciting update for our church
description: I really like it although I'm still in the set-up stage.
author: emily L.
pros: I love how fast and mobile-first the platform is. I have used everything ( I mean everything ) for our church site, Every platform you can imagine and yet my expectations have always fallen a little flat. Nucleus has so much of the ease of use, customization, photo filters and galleries that I have been looking for with an added prayer wall that works and the "flows" for communication and quick navigation of our views to get exactly where they want and quickly! The flows are the most exciting feature for me and even though I am still in the trial I am looking forward to working with Nucleus for our church long-term.
cons: I love so much about the site but a few features I wish you had were: More 1-click themes that include global styling, RSS feed for podcasts, and maybe I missed it since I'm still setting up but a great looking video page for our sermons that will automatically update from youtube and a good live section for our broadcast.
author_duration: Used the software for: I used a free trial

review_title: Worth it for every size of church
description: I have loved it since day 1. Support has always been there when I needed it. Building a new page takes so much less time than it would with my old website provider.
author: Dillon Y.
pros: The ease and ability to create a beautiful website without needing experts in the church. Small churches/church plants don’t always have someone like that available.
cons: Honestly, there’s just SO MUCH they do that it’s hard to keep up with the newest update or feature. What a great problem to have.

review_title: Nucleus is the best Church Website platform!
description: One of the best experience with Nucleus has been the ability to create a form for people to respond & sign up for events, etc. We also like that it allowed us to create a one-stop site for people to connect with us and us with them.
author: Carla B.
pros: I like the way the end product looks: we have a functional website that is easy for people to navigate and find out the information they are looking for. The card-based design results in a very user-friendly and device-adaptable format. It is adaptable to almost any situation that we need to either post information or to gather responses. Nucleus is easy to learn to use, and the customer support is fantastic. They are always right there to help us solve a technical issue. We are charter 'members', and have loved it from Day 1.
cons: I don't like that I can't use the Sermon Engine (Library) locations as the link to a Sermon Page/Card - I have to store the audio file off site, copy/paste that URL, etc. Since Sermon Engine is a direct upload of the audio file, why can't it be the source on a Sermon Card. When I post the weekly sermon, it involves creating 3 different web pages: the storage website, the Nucleus Sermon Card, and the Nucleus Sermon Engine. Watching for better integration in the future.

author: Josh B.
pros: Nucleus is the real deal! Their easy-to-use website builder, unlimited video library, and follow-up system called "flows" have completely transformed our church's digital presence. They've made it a breeze to upload sermons, engage with our congregation, and create an impressive online platform. We're beyond thrilled with Nucleus and their exceptional services.
cons: They are rocking the stage there is hardly any competitors. Nucleus for the win

review_title: Best in the business - consistently saves us time and money - more of a partner to us
author: Ed M.
pros: We started with Nucleus as a website management product. Then when they moved to online giving, they saved us so much money - the change paid for itself and the other products many times over.
cons: This is not really a negative because they haven't promised this as a feature. I wish it were also our Church Management System. They are so good at the other products I can only imagine they would be the best in business at this as well.

author: Cris B.
pros: The thing I love most is the customer service. Not only do I get a response usually within a hour, it is fixed right away and they are able to help and get me on my way.
cons: I it would integrate more with Planning Center. Since we use PCO people database, I don’t want to make our people have to create a second account on Nucleus and we have to manage them on two platforms.

review_title: Makes quality web design (and more) achievable for churches
author: Nathaniel K.
pros: I love that Nucleus empowers churches with the tools they need to navigate the online/digital world. Their products are robust, but still user-friendly to people with less experience in changing technology. Their customer service is top-of-the-line, and they are transparent. [sensitive content hidden] and others are great at educating their customers about the industry and tips through their video series and podcast. You can tell they have a heart for the local church in all they do.
cons: Nucleus 2 is feature-rich, and it's constantly being updated (a good thing), but that makes it a little overwhelming to look at the scope of what's possible to do versus what we actually need to do. So it can be daunting to start out, but the help articles are very thorough.

author: Corey A.
pros: Nucleus is by far my favorite web service for my church. We have tried clover, faith life, square space, Wix, and others [SENSITIVE CONTENT HIDDEN] and his team have done an incredible job! They also have free social posts every month, lots of b-role for sermon videos (free), and a really really good sermon hosting page! Plus they are not done. They keep rolling out features!! The back end of the website is super simple and easy to navigate. You would be wise to go with them for sure.
cons: No cons we did not experience anything we did not like

description: I am saving SO much time in administering our site. On top of that, our people are connecting and able to maneuver the site and find the forms they are looking for very easily. Also, their HELP section is FILLED with articles that are easy to read and apply regarding troubleshooting. If that doesn't work, their customer service is always there to resolve anything.
author: Tito S.
pros: Our Church, Tabernacle of God Church, has been only using Nucleus as our website building since week one of 2020 and I am so upset that we didn't jump on earlier. It is SO easy to set up and it's impossible to mess up. It's looks amazing and works so smooth. Our web engagement and our sign ups for first time guests, events, classes, and programs has DRASTICALLY increased! I've used other Church web builders and Word Press, and neither of them can compare. On top of that, the Sermon Engine, Media Clips and the Social Media Program that comes with Nucleus is just not fare. Nucleus is like the 90's NBA Dream Team dominating competitors!
cons: It was hard to sell our Church leaders on Nucleus because the company seems "new". Besides that, Nucleus has exceeded my expectations.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months

review_title: A Step Up in Church Websites
description: We love Nucleus - our church website has never looked better - it's designed with churches in mind whereas other similar platforms we've used are not.
author: Sammy D.
pros: It just works. And it just looks good. yes there is plenty to tinker with - but bottom line is input your content (and do so easily with their suggested pages etc) and boom. You've got a great-looking, functional, mobile-first website
cons: Jumping between draft pages and your live website isn't always easy to figure out which one you're in.

description: The Nucleus Team is very helpful and has continued to add value to the product. It now includes the sermon engine, nucleus media, and nucleus social.
author: Scott G.
pros: Nucleus is great at encouraging next steps. We are regularly finding new ways to get people engaged with their faith and our church using Nucleus.
cons: It would be nice to have an integration with Church Community Builder and MailerLite. RebelGive looks promising but doesn’t yet play as well as possible with our accounting software.

description: The customer service that I receive is outstanding. They reply quickly and go out of their way to make sure that all of my questions and concerns are answered and problems are solved!
author: Luke G.
pros: Easy to use, high-quality software, and has increased traffic for our institution by more than 10 times.
cons: There is less customization then I would like but that also benefits our team because it’s easy to maintain. It’s not really much of a con
author_profession: Associate Pastor of Next Generation Ministries

description: Their customer service is excellent. They are constantly updating the software making more things available to give customers more options.
author: Amy M.
pros: Nucleus is super easy to use. Even if you don't know anything about website design its easy. I love the drag and drop and the templates that are available.
cons: Since it is so easy to use, there isn't much flexibility in what you can do. Its very cookie cutter but it looks really clean.

author: Drew W.
pros: Nucleus is a top-tier software that actually cares about churches! We are saving over $6000 this year in fees that our previous software was HIDING in each transaction.
cons: Not an easy bulk import feature, but I understand the need for each user to set up their own account.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
